article_meta.title: Federal immigration officers board an elevator with respondents at immigration court in New York on 17 November 2025

reasoning: The response contains an attribution violation by flagging quoted source language as author bias, uses non-standard bias type labels, and includes some questionable flags. It does identify one genuine author-level loaded word choice (“spied”), but overall has moderate failures.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: It incorrectly flags a quoted statement from Brad Lander (“surveillance and intimidation by Trump's corrupted Justice Department...”) as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Several items are weak or invalid: a quoted source is treated as author bias; structural/placement claims are asserted without anchoring to specific author prose; and some flagged items are arguable rather than clear bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The major clear instance in author prose (“spied”) was caught; there were few unequivocal additional author-level biases missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels like “Loaded Language,” “Imbalance in Presentation,” “Framing,” “Placement Bias,” and “Selective Evidence” are not among the defined valid types, leading to multiple mislabelings.
